MEMORANDUM IN OPPOSITION TO PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION (TRANSACTION ID # 100042725) FILED BY RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT
DECLARATION OF WILLIAM M. QUINN, JR. IN OPPOSITION TO PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION (TRANSACTION ID # 100042725) FILED BY RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-02-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER UNITED EDUCATORS OF SAN FRANCISCO'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. THE ARBITRATION, HOWEVER, SHALL NOT INCLUDE THE ISSUE WHETHER RESPONDENT SFUSD VIOLATED ARTICLE 5.7 OF THE COLLECTIVE BARGAINING AGREEMENT. THE SCOPE OF ARBITRABILITY IS A QUESTION FOR THE COURT. (AT&T TECHNOLOGIES, INC. V. COMMUNICATION WORKERS OF AMERICA, 475 U.S. 643, 649 (1986).) PER ARTICLES 19.6, 19.8.3.3.1, AND 19.9.2 OF THE CBA, IT IS TOO LATE TO ADD THE ARTICLE 5.7 ISSUE TO THE ARBITRATION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE; HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/HK

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Aug-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Manuel A. Boigues of Weinberg, Roger & Rosenfeld, Tel. No. 1-510-337-1001
appearing for the Petitioner;
No other appearances on the matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Petitioner United Educators of San Francisco's petition to compel arbitration is granted. The arbitration, however, shall not include the issue whether respondent SFUSD violated Article 5.7 of the Collective Bargaining Agreement. The scope of arbitrability is a question for the court. (AT&T Technologies, Inc. v. Communication Workers of America, 475 U.S. 643, 649 (1986).) Per Articles 19.6, 19.8.3.3.1, and 19.9.2 of the CBA, it is too late to add the Article 5.7 issue to the arbitration. Order signed. Judge; Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk
